# Accounting for Share Capital - DETAILED NOTES

## Introduction
Accounting for share capital is a crucial aspect of financial accounting as it deals with the representation of ownership and capital structure of a company. The share capital is the lifeblood of every company, and its management affects the entire financial structure of the organization. In this chapter, we will delve into the various components and concepts related to accounting for share capital.

## What is Share Capital?
Share capital refers to the total amount of money contributed by the shareholders to a company in exchange for ownership shares. It represents the amount of capital that the company has raised from the public or its owners. Share capital is a vital component of a company's financial structure and plays a significant role in determining its financial position and performance.

## Components / Sub-topics

### Types of Share Capital
| Type of Share Capital | Definition | Characteristics |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Authorized Share Capital | The maximum amount of share capital that a company can issue as per its Memorandum of Association. | It is the maximum amount of capital that the company can raise. |
| Issued Share Capital | The actual amount of share capital that has been subscribed and issued by the company. | It is the amount of capital that has been raised from the public or shareholders. |
| Subscribed Share Capital | The amount of share capital that has been subscribed by the shareholders, but not yet issued. | It represents the amount of capital that has been committed by the shareholders, but not yet received by the company. |
| Paid-up Share Capital | The amount of share capital that has been actually received by the company from the shareholders. | It represents the actual amount of capital that has been received by the company from the shareholders. |

### Shares
| Type of Share | Definition | Characteristics |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Equity Shares | Shares that represent ownership in a company and are entitled to a share of the company's profits. | They are also known as ordinary shares and represent the ownership of the company. |
| Preference Shares | Shares that have a higher claim on assets and dividends than equity shares. | They are also known as preference stock and have a higher claim on assets and dividends than equity shares. |

## Authorized Share Capital
Authorized share capital is the maximum amount of share capital that a company can issue as per its Memorandum of Association. It is the maximum amount of capital that the company can raise. The authorized share capital is divided into different classes, such as equity shares, preference shares, and debentures.

### Characteristics of Authorized Share Capital
* It is the maximum amount of capital that the company can raise.
* It is the amount of capital that the company has authorized to issue.
* It is divided into different classes, such as equity shares, preference shares, and debentures.
* It is mentioned in the Memorandum of Association of the company.

## Issued Share Capital
Issued share capital is the actual amount of share capital that has been subscribed and issued by the company. It is the amount of capital that has been raised from the public or shareholders. The issued share capital is divided into different classes, such as equity shares, preference shares, and debentures.

### Characteristics of Issued Share Capital
* It is the actual amount of capital that has been raised from the public or shareholders.
* It is the amount of capital that has been issued by the company.
* It is divided into different classes, such as equity shares, preference shares, and debentures.
* It is mentioned in the Balance Sheet of the company.

## Subscribed Share Capital
Subscribed share capital is the amount of share capital that has been subscribed by the shareholders, but not yet issued. It represents the amount of capital that has been committed by the shareholders, but not yet received by the company. The subscribed share capital is divided into different classes, such as equity shares, preference shares, and debentures.

### Characteristics of Subscribed Share Capital
* It is the amount of capital that has been committed by the shareholders, but not yet received by the company.
* It is the amount of capital that has been subscribed by the shareholders.
* It is divided into different classes, such as equity shares, preference shares, and debentures.
* It is mentioned in the Balance Sheet of the company.

## Paid-up Share Capital
Paid-up share capital is the amount of share capital that has been actually received by the company from the shareholders. It represents the actual amount of capital that has been received by the company from the shareholders. The paid-up share capital is divided into different classes, such as equity shares, preference shares, and debentures.

### Characteristics of Paid-up Share Capital
* It is the actual amount of capital that has been received by the company from the shareholders.
* It is the amount of capital that has been paid up by the shareholders.
* It is divided into different classes, such as equity shares, preference shares, and debentures.
* It is mentioned in the Balance Sheet of the company.

## Ideal and Non-Ideal Scenarios
In the case of a company, the authorized share capital may not be the same as the issued share capital. The authorized share capital is the maximum amount of capital that the company can issue, while the issued share capital is the actual amount of capital that has been subscribed and issued by the company.

### Example
A company has an authorized share capital of ₹10,00,000, but it has issued only ₹5,00,000 worth of shares. In this case, the company has issued only 50% of its authorized share capital.

## Properties & Applications
The accounting for share capital is a crucial aspect of financial accounting as it deals with the representation of ownership and capital structure of a company. The share capital is the lifeblood of every company, and its management affects the entire financial structure of the organization.

### Importance of Share Capital
* It represents the ownership of the company.
* It affects the capital structure of the company.
* It affects the financial position and performance of the company.
* It represents the amount of capital that has been raised from the public or shareholders.

### Accounting Treatment
* Authorized share capital is shown in the Balance Sheet as a liability.
* Issued share capital is shown in the Balance Sheet as a liability.
* Subscribed share capital is shown in the Balance Sheet as a liability.
* Paid-up share capital is shown in the Balance Sheet as a liability.

### Conclusion
Accounting for share capital is a crucial aspect of financial accounting as it deals with the representation of ownership and capital structure of a company. The share capital is the lifeblood of every company, and its management affects the entire financial structure of the organization. In this chapter, we have discussed the various components and concepts related to accounting for share capital.
